title: crataegus monogyna var. ferox
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. ferox, a variant of the common hawthorn (Crataegus monogyna), is a plant species known for its thorny branches and small white flowers. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including cardiovascular issues, digestive problems, and anxiety disorders, due to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and reduce stress levels. Crataegus monogyna var. ferox can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
